data.description: User will drop a new background image into /public/images/ to replace the current low-quality hero-bg.png (1.4MB, used in Hero section). Update the asset path / references and verify rendering on mobile + desktop. Filename TBD when user provides the file.
data.title: Replace hero background photo with higher-quality version

data.body: Shipped: 4 AVIF (20.4/79.2/153.7/235.9 KB at 640/1280/1920/2560w) + 4 WebP (25.7/102.7/207.2/329.6 KB) + 960w PNG fallback (132.2 KB). All under budget. Hero.tsx now uses <picture> with AVIF/WebP/PNG sources. skyline4.png deleted. pnpm typecheck + build pass; tests show 7 pre-existing failures (same with my changes stashed). Note for user: the 52 MB source PNG at /Users/fractalos/Dev/fractal-nyc/public/images/fractal background.png (main repo, untracked) can be deleted once this PR merges — it's been copied to assets-src/ in this worktree (git-ignored). PNG fallback dropped from 1280w to 960w to fit ~200 KB target (palette PNG of a complex photo blew 250 KB at 1280w).

Review pass. Code matches plan; <picture> preserves opacity/transform; loading='eager' + fetchPriority='high' present; sizes='100vw' correct; meaningful alt text. Script is idempotent (cleans old variants), fails non-zero on budget overrun, documents the 960w PNG fallback deviation inline (palette-quantized photo blew 1280w budget — acceptable; 132 KB vs prior 1.4 MB skyline4). All 9 variants present in public/images/hero/ and dist/images/hero/ with sizes within ~3% of reported (640w AVIF 20.4KB / WebP 25.7KB through 2560w AVIF 235.9KB / WebP 329.6KB). skyline4.png deleted from repo and absent from dist/; no orphan references in src/ or index.html. sharp added as devDep, build:hero-bg script present and NOT wired into pnpm build. .gitignore excludes assets-src/; README.md is short and clear. typecheck clean; pnpm build clean; tests 141 pass / 7 pre-existing failures (neighborhood.test.tsx selector issues, unrelated to hero). PRD alignment: mobile-first 20.4 KB hero is a massive win for the 1.2-min 3G load. No inline fixes needed.
